etymology.description: 跑(跑) (pǎo, "run") indicates the action of running. 步(步) (bù, "step") refers to steps or walking, highlighting the movement aspect.
etymology.interpretation: 跑(跑) (pǎo, "run") depicts swift movement, so it naturally implies rapid foot motion. 步(步) (bù, "step") focuses on the basic unit of motion, a single step. Combining these, 跑步 (pǎobù) emphasizes the repeated, rapid steps characteristic of running or jogging.

examples.chinese: 我每天早上跑步(我每天早上跑步)。
跑步可以增强心肺功能(跑步可以增強心肺功能)。
在公园里跑步(在公園里跑步)，感觉非常舒适(感覺非常舒適)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gōngyuán lǐ pǎobù, gǎnjué fēicháng shūshì.
examples.english: Running in the park feels very comfortable.
